About Us

St. Mary’s Academy, Patna

St. Mary’s Academy is a Catholic school established and administered by the Catholic Archdiocese of Patna. This institution is established and administered under the rights and powers granted to religious and linguistic minorities by the constitution of India (Art. 29 and 30) the principal and other religious staff is appointed by the Archbishop from time to time. The school managing committee is responsible for the execution of the school rules and policy of Patna Diocesan Corporation.

Recent Activities

22 Jan

Our students presented their innovative projects at the Annual Science Fair 2026, exploring robotics, renewable energy, and environmental solutions.

20 Jan

The School Sports Day saw students competing in athletics, relay races, and team games, showcasing their sportsmanship and teamwork skills.

18 Jan

Students displayed their creativity in the Annual Art Exhibition, featuring paintings, sculptures, and digital artwork inspired by nature and school life.

12 Jan

The School Music Concert brought together talented students performing classical, contemporary, and folk music, entertaining the entire school community.

10 Jan

Our students participated in the Inter-School Debate Competition, presenting well-researched arguments on global issues and earning accolades for their communication skills.

function MissionVision() { return (

DPS society

The Delhi Public School Society took shape in 1949, with the long march to independence over and a need for young educated citizens to take India into the future. Guided by the cause of pioneering quality education for India’s deserving young minds, the society embarked on establishing many a premier school across the country. Today, the DPS family is reckoned as a stalwart in education. For more than six decades and Global Network of over 200 schools, DPS is probably the only school in the country with such credentials to its name

Read More

Vision

Commitment to fulfil the responsibility of meeting the contemporary challenges of education in the country and empower our students to be environment friendly citizen of the world.

Read More

Mission

Providing our students, cultural, linguistic, secular and technological education in order to promote harmony and spirit of Unity.

Read More
); } export default MissionVision;

{/* Title row */}

The
Mater Dei
Legacy

Mater Dei School for girls, was established in 1956, by the Sisters, Franciscan Missionaries of Mary. This Christian Minority School was begun with the primary objective of providing education in a Christian atmosphere to all children, especially Christians. Our aim is personal and integral formation of the young. We make special efforts towards the moral, spiritual, intellectual, social and physical development of children. The motto of our school is “To love through Truth’.

{/* First image */}
{/* Second image with play icon */}

History

Mater Dei school for girls, was established in 1956, by the Sisters, Franciscan Missionaries of Mary.

Vision

Mater Dei School seeks to be the enduring source of transformative education that remains dynamic and responsive to the ever evolving human race. We are a legacy where timeless values meet the future’s brightest potential. We envision a world where our graduate is a force for positive change, distinguished by a rare harmony of knowledge, character and innovative action.

Mission

Mater Dei School is a community dedicated to the holistic development of every student – nurturing their mind, character and inner life. Guided by a founding spirit of joyful simplicity and profound compassion, we commit to fostering global citizens of courage and integrity. Mater Dei empowers its students to embrace the complex 21st century world with innovative thinking and ethical purpose. We teach the wisdom of action: using knowledge to serve the marginalized and the vulnerable, and to build bridges of justice and peace.

About us

St. Xavier's collegiate School,
Kolkata

St. Xavier's Collegiate School, Kolkata, is administered by the Society of Jesus (Jesuits). The English Jesuits landed in Bengal in 1834 and started St. Xavier's School in Calcutta in 1836 for the European and Eurasian children of the wealthier classes. In November 1859, a group of seven Belgian Jesuits under Fr. Henri Depelchin, SJ took over St. John's School and renamed it St. Xavier's Collegiate School on the site of the burnt-down Sans Souci Theatre on Park Street.
Over more than 150 years, St. Xavier's has become one of India's leading educational institutions, known for academic rigor, holistic Jesuit education, discipline, leadership, and service. With nearly 3000 students from Lower Kindergarten to Class XII, the school continues to embrace the future with the spirit of "Nihil Ultra" (Nothing beyond).

GONZAGA HOUSE

Aloysius Gonzaga (1568 - 1591). Heir to the noble family title of Castiglione (North Italy), his father took him on traning expeditions to teach him the art of arms. He died as a Jesuit student at the age of 23, nursing the plague-striken.

Mr Ranjit Bhattacharya

britto house

John de Britto (1647 - 1693), nick-named the 'Martyr' at the court of Portugal, became a 'Pandara Swami' in Madurai. He was executed for his faith at Oriyur, in the district of Ramnad, in Tamil Nadu.

Mr Siddhartha Das

loyola house

Ignatius Loyola (1491 - 1556), a Basque (Spaniard) and soldier-saint who whilst convalescing after his leg was shattered in a battle against the French, conceived a burning desire to follow in the footsteps of Christ.

Ms. Jennifer Ahmed

berchmans house

John Berchmans (1599 - 1621), a Belgian, hailed from a family of cobblers. Thanks to a benefector, he completed his school studies and joined the Jusuits at the age of 17. He was still a student, when he died of ill-health.

Mr. Brett Fernandes

bow bazar

loreto day school

Mary Ward, an English woman, founded the Institute of the Blessed Virgin Mary (IBVM) to serve the church, “through education and other works congruous to the times”. This Religious order, now spread through the world, engages itself in many works...

Loreto Bowbazar was established in 1844 on the 8th of December. It was known as St. Francis Xavier Convent. It was originally a small private school owned by Mr. & Mrs. Cooper. Comprising boarders from the middle income group, who paid fees. A Free School was also running parallel to the paid school. There were both boys and girls studying in the school. In 1845 the Christian Brothers took charge of the boys. Loreto Bowbazar was the second assignment for the young Loreto Sisters who came to India from Ireland and the Cathedral of the Most Holy Rosary 15, Portuguese Church Street, Kolkata 700001 was their first port of call after reaching India in 1841, on the 30th of December. In 1845 the Loreto Sisters were asked to help in the Medical College Hospital temporarily. The Sisters managed the domestic & household duties. They managed the provisions & looked after the ladies in the Female Ward. This arrangement proved to be economical for the doctors. In 1857 the year of the Mutiny, there was a rumour that trouble might spread to Calcutta. The people from the suburbs were removed to Fort William area and the orphans and Sisters from Loreto Entally were moved to Loreto Bowbazar.

About us

Mount Carmel School,
Hearunganj

We the sisters of Mother of Carmel, inspired by the life and teaching of JESUS CHRIST stand for the intellectual, social, economic moral and spiritual advancement of people especially women and children through education. The school aims at the academic excellence, development of skills and character formation based on the love of God and the service of mankind as modelled in Jesus Christ, who was an educator par excellence. Human excellence being the priority we strive hard to form the children into well integrated, mature, patriotic and socially committed human beings, who will work for profound social transformation. Our mission is to facilitate human excellence in students by blending study and personality development with cultural, artistic, religious and social activities, in the well knitted yearly routine of Mount Carmel School. The prevailing environment of student friendly approach and personal care helps each pupil to discover and improve one's inherent gifts, and developing one's moral sensitivity and sense of God.

BASE_URL + item.attachments[0] : "", title: item.title, }; }); setHighlightsData(formatted) } fetchHighlight(); },[]); const dummy_highlight=[ { src:"", title:"Stay tuned for latest updates", desc: "", dateText: "", }, ]; const filteredHighlights = highlightsData.length === 0 ? dummy_highlight : highlightsData; return ( <> {filteredHighlights.map((item,index,arr) => (
